text: Therefore, exploring the behind mechanisms of Prdx1 against SAP become the focus of subsequent work. Our previous works have showed that Prdx1 deficiency led to a marked increase in pancreatic cell death[26]. And consistently, in this work, we found that Prdx1 overexpression resulted in significantly reduced cell death (Figure S2A). Given that apoptosis and ferroptosis are well-established cell death pathways in acinar cells during pancreatitis, we next performed IF to identify the predominant forms of cell death occurring in these tissues. Prdx1 expression in acinar cells notably attenuated both cell apoptosis and ferroptosis, as evidenced by results shown in Figure S2A. Conversely, knockout of Prdx1 promotes cell death (Figure S2B). To further validation, we performed immunohistochemical staining for additional key apoptotic markers, specifically cleaved caspase 6 and cleaved caspase 8 in flox, PcKO, AAV8-ctrl, and AAV8-Prdx1 groups. Consistent with our previous findings, images presented in Figure S2C confirmed the reduction of these crucial factors upon Prdx1 overexpression, conversely, Prdx1 knockout elevated expression of cleaved caspase 6 and caspase 8. Taken together, these data strongly demonstrated that Prdx1 exerted a protective effect in acinar cells by inhibiting both apoptosis and ferroptosis. These results compellingly suggested that promoting endogenous Prdx1 production within acinar cells was a promising therapeutic strategy for improving SAP pathology. However, it’s important to note that effective pharmacological interventions specifically targeting this mechanism or enhancing acinar Prdx1 levels are currently unavailable, underscoring the urgent need for the discovery and development of novel therapeutic agents in this area.
heading_context: 3.1 Intracellular Prdx1 deficiency in acinar cells exacerbates SAP by inducing cell apoptosis and ferroptosis
